title: Obsidian Export

story.i_want_to: export the knowledge wiki as Obsidian-compatible linked markdown files
story.so_that: I can browse the knowledge graph locally in Obsidian with full backlink navigation and graph view

rationale: Obsidian is the de facto standard for interlinked markdown knowledge bases among developers. Exporting the knowledge wiki as Obsidian-compatible files with YAML frontmatter and [[wikilink]] backlinks lets developers leverage Obsidian's powerful graph view, backlink navigation, and local-first search. This bridges the API-native loopctl knowledge store with the developer's preferred knowledge management tool, making the accumulated knowledge immediately useful outside the API.

acceptance_criteria.description: GET /api/v1/projects/:project_id/knowledge/export endpoint exists (role: user+). Returns a ZIP file (Content-Type: application/zip) containing markdown files for all published articles in the specified project.
GET /api/v1/knowledge/export endpoint exists (role: user+). Returns a ZIP file containing markdown files for all published articles across the tenant (tenant-wide export).

ZIP file structure organizes files by category: `{category}/{slugified-title}.md`. Category directories use lowercase names matching the enum values (pattern/, convention/, decision/, finding/, reference/).
Each markdown file includes YAML frontmatter with fields: title, category, tags (as YAML list), status, source_type (if present), created_at (ISO 8601), updated_at (ISO 8601).

ArticleLinks are rendered as Obsidian [[wikilinks]] in a '## Related Articles' section at the end of each file. The link text uses the target article's title: `[[Target Title]]`. Link types are annotated: `[[Target Title]] (contradicts)` for contradiction links.
A `_index.md` file is included at the root of the ZIP listing all articles grouped by category, with [[wikilinks]] to each article. Format: category heading, then bulleted list of article links.
Only published articles are included in the export. Draft, archived, and superseded articles are excluded.

Special characters in filenames are handled: slashes, colons, question marks, asterisks, quotes, angle brackets, pipes are stripped or replaced with hyphens in the slugified title. Multiple consecutive hyphens are collapsed to one.
The ZIP is generated in-memory using Erlang's :zip module (no temp files on disk). Response includes Content-Disposition header with filename `knowledge-export-{date}.zip`.
Both endpoints enforce tenant scoping via RLS. A tenant can only export their own articles.
Returns 200 with empty ZIP (containing only _index.md) if no published articles exist, rather than 404.

test_cases.name: Export project knowledge as ZIP with correct structure
preconditions: fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id, status: :published, category: :pattern, title: "GenServer Pooling"}) as article
expected_results: Response 200 with Content-Type: application/zip
ZIP contains pattern/genserver-pooling.md
File has YAML frontmatter with title, category, tags, status, created_at, updated_at
File body contains the article body text

test_cases.name: Export includes Obsidian backlinks for related articles
preconditions: Two published articles with a relates_to ArticleLink between them
steps: Unzip and read both markdown files
expected_results: Each file has a '## Related Articles' section
Section contains [[Target Title]] wikilink to the other article
Wikilink text matches the target article's exact title

test_cases.name: Export excludes non-published articles
steps: List all files in the ZIP
expected_results: ZIP contains only one article file (plus _index.md)
Draft and archived articles are not present

test_cases.name: Export handles special characters in titles
steps: Check the filename in the ZIP
expected_results: Filename is slugified: what-why-decision-important.md
No illegal filesystem characters in the path
YAML frontmatter title preserves the original title

test_cases.name: Tenant isolation: cannot export another tenant's articles
expected_results: ZIP contains only _index.md (no article files)
tenant_b's articles are not included

test_cases.name: Index file groups articles by category
expected_results: _index.md contains category headings: Pattern, Convention, Decision
Each heading has bulleted [[wikilinks]] to articles in that category
Categories with no articles are omitted

test_cases.name: Empty export returns ZIP with only index file
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant with no published articles
expected_results: Response 200 with valid ZIP
ZIP contains only _index.md
_index.md indicates no articles available

technical_notes: Use Erlang's `:zip.create/3` with the `:memory` option to generate ZIPs in-memory without temp files. Pattern: `{:ok, {_filename, zip_binary}} = :zip.create('export.zip', file_list, [:memory])` where file_list is `[{'path/file.md', binary_content}, ...]`.

Slugification: downcase, replace spaces and special chars with hyphens, collapse multiple hyphens, trim leading/trailing hyphens. Consider using a simple regex-based approach rather than adding a slugify dependency.
ArticleLinks must be preloaded with their target articles to render [[wikilinks]]. Use a single query with joins rather than N+1 queries.
For the project-scoped endpoint, include articles where project_id matches OR project_id is nil (tenant-wide articles are included in all project exports).
Testing: async: true, ConnCase, use Erlang's :zip.unzip/2 to verify ZIP contents in tests, fixture(:article) with various statuses and categories, tenant isolation test.
